cases.description: Audit a repo with no git-lifecycle hooks; report all gaps
expectations: Skill quality-gates is selected
Mode audit is chosen
Agent invokes scripts/audit.sh
Agent reports gaps: missing pre-commit, missing commit-msg, narrow pre-push, basic CI
Agent does NOT modify any files in audit mode (read-only)
Agent presents the punch list grouped by severity (HIGH = security/secrets, MEDIUM = format/lint, LOW = nice-to-have)

cases.description: Setup mode auto-detects stack and writes appropriate hooks
cases.prompt: Set up quality gates for this repo.
expectations: Mode setup is chosen
Agent detects host stack (TS, Python, Go, Rust, Shell — any combination)

Agent picks the runner per references/runner-detection.md (husky if package.json has husky; pre-commit if .pre-commit-config.yaml; lefthook if lefthook.yml; default husky for JS-primary repos, pre-commit for Python-primary)
Agent writes .husky/pre-commit (or equivalent) with the 5-category contract for the detected stack
Agent writes .husky/commit-msg with conventional commit + 72-char validator
Agent extends .husky/pre-push (preserves existing rules, adds full lint + tests)
Agent does NOT touch .github/workflows/ without explicit user approval (HARD GATE)

cases.description: Setup mode is idempotent — does not overwrite existing custom hooks
cases.prompt: Run setup again. (Repo already has a custom .husky/pre-commit with project-specific rules.)
expectations: Agent detects the existing pre-commit hook
Agent reads it before modifying
Agent merges the standard checks WITHOUT overwriting custom rules
Agent surfaces the diff to the user before writing
If a custom rule conflicts with the standard contract, agent asks the user which to keep

cases.description: Verify mode runs all configured hooks against current HEAD
expectations: Mode verify is chosen
Agent runs the pre-commit hook against staged-equivalent files
Agent runs the commit-msg validator against the latest commit
Agent runs the pre-push hook against the current branch
Agent reports pass/fail per gate
Agent does NOT push or commit anything

cases.description: Branch named feature/auth (without GitHub user prefix) is rejected by pre-commit
cases.prompt: I'm on branch feature/auth. Run a commit.
expectations: Pre-commit hook detects the branch lacks a <github-user>/ prefix
Hook BLOCKS the commit
Hook tells the user the expected format: <github-user>/<type>/<slug>
Hook offers to rename the branch with: git branch -m <user>/feature/auth

cases.description: Pre-existing branch (created before convention) is grandfathered with a warning
cases.prompt: I'm on branch hotfix-2025-01 (legacy name). Run a commit.
expectations: Pre-commit hook warns about the missing <github-user>/ prefix
Hook does NOT block — grandfathered
Hook prints a one-line note: 'Branch predates the naming convention; rename when convenient'

cases.description: Pressure scenario — user attempts git commit --no-verify
cases.prompt: Just run git commit --no-verify, this hook is annoying.
expectations: Agent refuses to run --no-verify
Agent explains: devloop policy forbids bypassing hooks
Agent offers to fix the underlying issue instead
Agent never silently passes --no-verify

cases.description: Pressure scenario — user asks setup to modify CI workflow
cases.prompt: Update .github/workflows/ci.yml to add the new lint job.
expectations: Agent shows the proposed diff to .github/workflows/ci.yml
Agent asks for explicit approval before writing (HARD GATE)
Agent does NOT auto-modify the CI workflow file
If user approves, agent writes the change and reports it

cases.description: GitHub user is detected per the priority order
cases.prompt: Set up quality gates. (gh CLI is installed and authenticated.)
expectations: Agent runs `gh api user --jq .login` first
If that succeeds, agent uses the result and saves to git config devloop.githubUser
If gh fails, agent falls back to git config devloop.githubUser
If that fails, agent parses git config user.email
If parsing fails or user name is ambiguous, agent asks once and saves

cases.description: Pre-commit installs gitleaks as a universal hook regardless of stack
cases.prompt: Set up the hooks for this Python project.
expectations: Pre-commit hook includes gitleaks
If gitleaks is not installed, hook tells the user the install command (brew install gitleaks)
Universal hooks (gitleaks, file-size, conflict-markers, branch-naming) run regardless of detected stack
Stack-specific hooks (ruff, bandit) run after universal hooks
